content.cn_text: 大家早上好。今天的讲座是关于一种不寻常蟹类在北方水域出现并产生越来越大影响的内容。
content.en_text: Good morning, everyone. Today's lecture is about the arrival and growing influence of

content.cn_text: 我会先讲一些总体观察，
content.en_text: an unusual crab species in northern waters. I'll begin with some general observations

content.cn_text: 然后再讲它们的行为、影响和长期管理。首先，当地渔民说，
content.en_text: before moving into behaviour, impacts and long-term management. To start, local fishermen say

content.cn_text: 这种生物开始出现在以前从未记录到它们的地点。
content.en_text: the creature has started showing up in spots where it was never recorded before.

content.cn_text: 安放在几只个体身上的追踪装置显示，它们最初来自一个
content.en_text: Cling devices placed on several individuals revealed they originally travelled from an

content.cn_text: 更靠南的地区，不过它们离开那个地区的原因仍有争议。
content.en_text: area located further south, although the reasons for leaving that region are still debated.

content.cn_text: 一些海洋学家认为，水层变化可能促成了这种新的迁移。
content.en_text: Some oceanographers believe changes in water layers may have encouraged this new movement.

content.cn_text: 随着位置变化，一个令人担忧的趋势是章鱼数量明显下降，
content.en_text: Along with the shift in location, one worrying trend is the noticeable drop in octopuses,

content.cn_text: 每当这种蟹的数量增加时，章鱼就会迅速消失。
content.en_text: which disappear quickly whenever the crab population increases.

content.cn_text: 潜水团队报告说，在他们通常的路线中，章鱼目击次数减少了，甚至在那些
content.en_text: Dive groups have reported fewer sightings on their usual routes, even in places that

content.cn_text: 过去一直很容易看到章鱼的地方也是如此。导游说游客经常问为什么这些聪明的
content.en_text: used to be reliable. Guides say visitors often ask why these clever

content.cn_text: 动物越来越难被发现。接下来，港口工作人员有时会
content.en_text: animals are becoming harder to spot. Moving on, people who work at the harbour sometimes

content.cn_text: 发现它脱落的壳碎片，通常是在暴风雨后被冲上岸。这些碎片摸起来
content.en_text: find discarded pieces of its shell, usually washed up after storms. These fragments feel

content.cn_text: 出奇地密实，比大多数人预想的重得多。它的质地非常粗糙坚硬，
content.en_text: surprisingly dense when handled, much heavier than most expect. The texture is so rugged

content.cn_text: 以至于有人把它比作建筑材料，而不像是来自生物的东西。
content.en_text: that some compare it to building material rather than something from a living creature.

content.cn_text: 此外，幼蟹往往停留在浅水湾，因为这些地方
content.en_text: In addition to this, younger crabs tend to remain in shallow inlets because these places

content.cn_text: 提供了躲避大型捕食者的藏身之处。海藻丛和岩石边缘形成小型
content.en_text: offer hiding spots from larger predators. Seaweed clusters and rock edges create small

content.cn_text: 隐蔽空间，让它们可以安全休息。只有在它们变得更强壮之后，才开始移向
content.en_text: pockets where they can rest safely. Only after they grow stronger do they begin moving toward

content.cn_text: 更深的水道。当我们观察一些北方村庄中它们的移动模式时，人们
content.en_text: deeper channels. When we look at their movement patterns in some northern villages, people

content.cn_text: 注意到大群蟹经过后，水边会留下长长的痕迹。
content.en_text: have noticed long tracks along the waters edge after large groups have passed through.

content.cn_text: 这些痕迹经常出现在裸露的海岸线上，尤其是在潮汐强烈的地方。
content.en_text: These patterns often appear on exposed coastlines, especially in places where tides are strong.

content.cn_text: 摄影师有时会等待这些罕见的聚集场景，以拍摄特别的照片。随着
content.en_text: Photographers sometimes wait for these rare gatherings to capture unusual images. As the

content.cn_text: 一年推进到较冷的月份，当地工作人员常常改变工作安排，
content.en_text: year progresses during the colder months, local crews often change their work routines

content.cn_text: 因为这些动物变得更难找到。随着冬季临近，蟹通常会转移到
content.en_text: because the animals become harder to find. As winter approaches, the crabs usually shift

content.cn_text: 更暗、更深且条件更稳定的水域。这种移动使研究人员
content.en_text: into darker, deeper waters where conditions are more stable. This movement makes it challenging

content.cn_text: 很难继续监测它们。现在，说到人类活动，许多船员
content.en_text: for researchers to continue monitoring them. Now, in terms of human activity, a number of crews

content.cn_text: 提到船上的某些物品在日常工作中造成了意外延误。
content.en_text: mentioned that certain items on board have been causing unexpected delays during routine work.

content.cn_text: 一个反复出现的问题涉及渔网，它们经常回来时已经处于需要
content.en_text: One recurring issue involves the nets, which often return in a condition that requires

content.cn_text: 额外时间整理的状态。因此，一些团队已经开始试验新设备，
content.en_text: extra time to sort out. Because of this, some teams have begun experimenting with new equipment

content.cn_text: 以避免中断。与此同时，该地区几个群体一直在讨论近期
content.en_text: to avoid interruptions. At the same time, several groups in the area have been debating how recent

content.cn_text: 海洋变化可能如何影响岸上的不同活动。有些人认为这些变化最终可能
content.en_text: changes in the sea might influence different activities on shore. Some believe these shifts could

content.cn_text: 影响更广泛的经济，尽管没人能就结果会是什么样达成一致。
content.en_text: eventually reach the wider economy, though no one agrees on what that outcome might look like.

content.cn_text: 少数人仍保持乐观，认为随着时间推移可能会出现意想不到的好处。
content.en_text: A few remain optimistic, suggesting that unexpected benefits could appear over time.

content.cn_text: 最后，几十年前的文件描述了一次精心组织的行动，将
content.en_text: Finally, documents from decades ago describe a carefully organized operation that introduced

content.cn_text: 这一物种引入陌生水域。根据这些记录，一组工程师负责
content.en_text: the species into unfamiliar waters. According to those notes, a group of engineers handled the

content.cn_text: 该行动早期阶段的实际操作。他们参与所带来的长期影响
content.en_text: practical side of the initiative during its early stages. The long-term effects of their involvement

content.cn_text: 当时并未被考虑。近年来，几个研究小组审查了
content.en_text: were not considered at the time. In recent years, several research groups have reviewed

content.cn_text: 关于该物种如何扩散的新数据。许多科学家认为，一些周边
content.en_text: new data about how the species is spreading. A number of scientists argue that some surrounding

content.cn_text: 栖息地如果某些趋势持续下去，可能会面临困难。另一些人认为，情况可以通过
content.en_text: habitats may struggle if certain trends continue. Others think the situation can be stabilized

content.cn_text: 持续监测和定期调整得到稳定。今天的讲座到此结束。感谢大家
content.en_text: with consistent monitoring and periodic adjustments. That concludes today's talk. Thank you for

content.cn_text: 收听。
content.en_text: listening.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Question 31–40

items.title: The Red King Crab is migrating into European waters from the far 31. _________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第31题问红帝王蟹从遥远的哪里迁入欧洲水域。原文说 originally travelled from an area located further south，所以答案是 south。

items.title: Its rapid spread threatens local marine species such as 32. _________, which it preys on.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第32题问红帝王蟹的快速扩散威胁哪种当地海洋物种。原文说 noticeable drop in octopuses，所以答案是 octopuses。

items.title: The Red King Crab has a deep red 33. _________, that develops after several molts.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第33题问红帝王蟹有深红色的什么。原文说 pieces of its shell，并描述其质地，所以答案是 shell。

items.title: Juvenile crabs often remain in 34. _________ waters where predators are fewer.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第34题问幼蟹通常停留在什么水域。原文说 younger crabs tend to remain in shallow inlets，所以答案是 shallow。

items.title: When travelling in large groups, the crabs damage long stretches of 35. _________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第35题问它们大群移动时会破坏长段什么。原文说 long tracks 出现在 exposed coastlines，所以答案是 coastlines。

items.title: During 36. _________, they tend to move into deeper water.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第36题问在哪个季节它们倾向于进入更深水域。原文说 As winter approaches, the crabs shift into deeper waters，所以答案是 winter。

items.title: Fishermen complain that their 37. _________ are frequently damaged.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第37题问渔民抱怨什么经常受损。原文说 recurring issue involves the nets，所以答案是 nets。

items.title: Some communities are concerned about the effect on their local 38. _________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第38题问一些社区担心其对当地什么的影响。原文说 influence different activities on shore，所以答案是 shore。

items.title: In the 1960s, Soviet 39. _________ intentionally introduced the Red King Crab to boost fishery production.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第39题问20世纪60年代苏联什么人有意引入红帝王蟹。原文说 a group of engineers handled the practical side of the initiative，所以答案是 engineers。

items.title: Today, environmental 40. _________ warn that its ecological impact may be greater than expected.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第40题问如今环境方面的谁警告其生态影响可能比预期更大。原文说 a number of scientists argue that some surrounding habitats may struggle，所以答案是 scientists。

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
The Red King Crab
Introduction
• The Red King Crab is migrating into European waters from the far $31$.
• Its rapid spread threatens local marine species such as $32$, which it preys on.
Physical characteristics
• The Red King Crab has a deep red $33$, that develops after several molts.
• Juvenile crabs often remain in $34$ waters where predators are fewer.
Migration behaviour
• When travelling in large groups, the crabs damage long stretches of $35$.
• During $36$, they tend to move into deeper water.
Impact on people
• Fishermen complain that their $37$ are frequently damaged.
• Some communities are concerned about the effect on their local $38$.
Management and control
• In the 1960s, Soviet $39$ intentionally introduced the Red King Crab to boost fishery production.
• Today, environmental $40$ warn that its ecological impact may be greater than expected.
